data.title: Analysts See Weekly Decline in U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

U.S. crude oil inventories likely saw a modest decline last week, while gasoline stocks are expected to continue building, according to a survey by The Wall Street Journal.

Commercial crude stocks are seen falling by 500,000 barrels to 421.9 million barrels in the week ended Jan. 16, according to the average estimate of nine analysts and traders. Five expect an increase and four predict a decline, with forecasts ranging from a build of 2 million barrels to a draw of 6.5 million barrels.

Gasoline inventories are expected to have risen by 1 million barrels to 252 million barrels, with estimates ranging from a 3 million barrel increase to a 2.6 million barrel drop.

Stocks of distillate fuel, mostly diesel, are expected to be down by 500,000 barrels at 128.7 million barrels, according to the survey. Forecasts range from a 2.7 million barrel drop to a 2 million barrel increase.

Refinery capacity use likely fell to 94.6% from 95.3% the week before, with forecasts ranging from a 0.7 percentage point increase to a 2.4 percentage point decline. Two analysts didn't forecast refinery runs.

The inventory data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration is scheduled for release Thursday at 12 p.m. EST.

 
                                    Crude    Gasoline  Distillates Refinery Use 
   Again Capital                    -1.6        2.6        1.8        0.7 
   Confluence Investment Management  2.0        0.5       -1.0       -0.8 
   Rystad Energy                    -6.5        0.9       -2.3       -2.4 
   Excel Futures                     1.4       -0.8       -1.6       -1.9 
   Spartan Capital Securities       -1.9       -2.6       -2.7        n/f 
   Mizuho                           -2.5        2.0        1.0       -0.5 
   Price Futures Group               2.0        2.0        2.0       unch 
   Ritterbusch and Associates        1.5        3.0       -1.3       -0.3 
   Tradition Energy                  0.8        1.5       -0.8        n/f 
 
   AVERAGE                          -0.5        1.0       -0.5       -0.7

Note: Numbers in millions of barrels, with the exception of refinery use, which is in percentage points.

n/f = no forecast

unch = unchanged

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: AI company Eightfold sued for helping companies secretly score job seekers
data.description:
Key points:
  • Job seekers say AI tools violate credit reporting laws
  • Two women seek to represent a nationwide class of applicants
  • One-third of Eightfold customers are Fortune 500 companies

By Jody Godoy

Eightfold AI, a venture capital-backed artificial intelligence hiring platform used by Microsoft , PayPal and many other Fortune 500 companies, is being sued in California for allegedly compiling reports used to screen job applicants without their knowledge.

The lawsuit filed on Tuesday accusing Eightfold of violating the Fair Credit Reporting Act shows how consumer advocates are seeking to apply existing law to AI systems capable of drawing inferences about individuals based on vast amounts of data.

Santa Clara, California-based Eightfold provides tools that promise to speed up the hiring process by assessing job applicants and predicting whether they would be a good fit for a job using massive amounts of data from online resumes and job listings. But candidates who apply for jobs at companies that use those tools are not given notice and a chance to dispute errors, job applicants Erin Kistler and Sruti Bhaumik allege in their proposed class action.

Because of that, they claim Eightfold violated the FCRA and a California law that gives consumers the right to view and challenge credit reports used in lending and hiring.

"There is no AI-exemption to these laws, which have for decades been an essential tool in protecting job applicants from abuses by third parties—like background check companies—that profit by collecting information about and evaluating job applicants," they said in the lawsuit.

A spokesperson for Eightfold did not immediately reply to a request for comment. Eightfold is backed by venture capital firms including SoftBank Vision Fund and General Catalyst.

Kistler and Bhaumik sued in California state court on behalf of all U.S. job seekers who applied for jobs and were evaluated using the company's tools. Labor law firm Outten & Golden and nonprofit advocacy group Towards Justice represent the proposed class.

Eightfold creates talent profiles of job seekers that include personality descriptions such as "team player" and "introvert," rank their "quality of education," and predict their future titles and companies, according to the lawsuit.

Kistler applied to roles at several companies that use Eightfold, including PayPal, and Bhaumik applied to companies including Microsoft, according to the complaint. Both hold science or tech degrees and have more than 10 years of experience. Neither was hired, and both believe that Eightfold's tools played a role.

Microsoft and PayPal are not defendants in the lawsuit. A Microsoft spokesperson declined to comment. A spokesperson for PayPal did not immediately reply to a request for comment.

One-third of Eightfold customers are Fortune 500 companies, including Salesforce and Bayer , according to the company's website. The New York State Department of Labor and Colorado Department of Labor and Employment also offer Eightfold-powered platforms for job seekers.

data.title: Volkswagen Posts Better-Than-Expected Cash Flow, Liquidity
data.description:
(17:54 GMT) *Volkswagen Automotive Division 2025 Net Cash Flow Around EUR6 Bln
(17:54 GMT) *Volkswagen: Figure Rose From EUR5 Bln in 2024
(17:55 GMT) *Volkswagen: Automotive Net Liquidity Rose to More Than EUR34 Bln at Dec. 31
(17:55 GMT) *Volkswagen: Figures Are Preliminary
(17:56 GMT) *Volkswagen Had Seen Automotive Net Cash Flow at EUR0 Bln For 2025
(17:56 GMT) *Volkswagen: Better Results Than Expected Due To Lower Working Capital, Lower-Than Expected Capex, R&D Costs
(18:14 GMT) Volkswagen Posts Better-Than-Expected Cash Flow, Liquidity

By Mauro Orru

Volkswagen reported better-than-expected net cash flow and net liquidity in its automotive division for last year, citing lower working capital and lower-than-anticipated capital expenditure and research-and-development investments.

The German carmaker said on Wednesday that its automotive business generated net cash flow of roughly 6 billion euros ($7.04 billion) in 2025, according to preliminary figures, compared with 5 billion euros a year earlier. The metric is above company expectations of around breakeven net cash flow.

The company also said net liquidity in the automotive division increased to more than 34 billion euros at the end of the year from 31 billion euros at the end of September, above company expectations of around 30 billion euros.

Meanwhile, Volkswagen said its investment ratio for the year is at around 12% of sales in the automotive division.

The company is scheduled to report its full set of 2025 results on March 10.

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com

data.title: Analysts Expect 103 Billion-Cubic-Foot Draw in U.S. Natural Gas Inventories
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

U.S. natural gas inventories likely saw a second consecutive below-average withdrawal last week, increasing the size of the storage surplus after a relatively mild start to January, according to a survey by The Wall Street Journal.

Natural gas in underground storage likely fell by 103 billion cubic feet to 3,082 Bcf in the week ended Jan. 16, according to the average estimate of 11 analysts, brokers and traders. Estimates range from a withdrawal of 76 Bcf to a draw of 116 Bcf.

The expected decline is smaller than the five-year average for the week of 191 Bcf, and would increase the surplus over the average to 194 Bcf from 106 Bcf the week before.

Storage draws are widely expected to increase in the following weeks, however, as extreme cold forecast for the second half of January is seen raising heating demand and possibly causing production freeze-offs.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release weekly natural gas storage data on Thursday at 10:30 a.m. EST.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: 10-Year, Silver, Crude Hit Inflection Points. What The Charts Say About 3 Macro Indicators. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Doug Busch

The stock market often telegraphs its next phase through macro instruments, and the current behavior of the 10-year Treasury yield, silver, and crude oil warrant close attention.

The S&P 500 fell 2.1% on Tuesday, undercutting its 50-day simple moving average for the third time in as many months. When that occurs in such a compressed time frame, concerns about risk appetite naturally arise. The surge in volatility didn't help. The VIX rallied 25% to start the week, vaulting above its 200-day simple moving average and approaching the very round $20 number.

Against that backdrop, it's worth examining the technical picture of the 10-year Treasury yield, silver, and crude oil, and what signals they may be sending.

The 10-year Treasury yield is closely watched for several reasons. It affects borrowing costs, influences investor risk appetite, and can serve as a gauge of inflation expectations. Tuesday's break above a bullish inverse head and shoulders pattern could signal the start of an equity pullback as rising rates tend to pressure stocks. The CBOE 10 Year Treasury Note Yield Index jumped 1.5% Tuesday, marking the second consecutive gap up session after Friday's 1.7% advance. The index also broke above its 200-day simple moving average for the first time since undercutting it on Aug. 1, when it tumbled 3.2%. The 1o-year's climb above 4.2% was significant as that level acted as support last summer and as stiff resistance in September, December, and earlier this month. This opens the path toward 4.5% later in the first half of 2026.

The 10-year Treasury yield was trading around 4.28% Wednesday.

Silver has been the standout among precious metals, and for good reason. Over the past year, the iShares Silver Trust has surged more than 200%, more than doubling the gain of the SPDR Gold Shares over the same period. While I'm not calling for an end to this impressive run, a tactical pullback before a continuation of the uptrend makes sense. Spot silver is trading well above both its 50-day simple moving average and 21-day exponential moving average, and the approach to the very round $100 level for the first time ever suggests a pause may be warranted. Notable caution flags include a bearish engulfing candle and dark cloud cover at the $80 level, followed by a bearish evening star completed last Friday. A sale here seems prudent, with a potential pullback into the $80 bull flag breakout area before a resumption toward $130 later in the year.

Silver was trading around $93 Wednesday.

Crude oil has been one of the only commodities to not get the rally memo. I believe that is about to change. The conventional wisdom following the capture of Venezuela President Nicolás Maduro was that oil was going to plunge from all the new supply coming online. That hasn't materialized. Crude has made a triple bottom near the $55/barrel area, a logical area to play against, and admire how the 21-day exponential moving average has crossed above the 50-day simple moving average and it is now holding above both. The last time that occurred, in June, oil prices rallied. I like how volume has been consistent since the start of 2026, suggesting some institutions taking a bullish stance. Big volume that came and went quickly last June and October couldn't buoy the price upward. This time feels different. Look for crude to rise toward $67 in the near term. Remain bullish above $57.50/barrel.

Crude oil was trading around $60.40/barrel Wednesday.

Doug Busch is the senior technical analyst at Barron's Investor Circle . His technical view is added to stock picks, including those published exclusively for Investor Circle readers. A glossary of technical terms is updated regularly with new entries.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Wells Fargo to Relocate Wealth Management Business to South Florida — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kenneth Corbin

Wells Fargo is planning to relocate the base of operations of its wealth management unit to south Florida later this year, the bank confirms. The initial move will involve roughly 100 staffers, primarily senior executives. They are moving to a new complex in West Palm Beach, making it the first major bank to shift its wealth operations to Florida, as noted by Bloomberg, which first reported the news.

Wells Fargo Advisors emerged from the acquisition of Wachovia Securities, which became part of Wells Fargo in 2008. The division will retain staff in its St. Louis headquarters and operations in New York and North Carolina, Wells Fargo says.

Wells Fargo's decision to shift its wealth headquarters to Florida follows a steady migration of investment advisors, businesses, and wealthy individuals to the Sunshine State, which has been promoting itself as a pro-business environment and has no individual income tax. In a survey last year of the fastest-growing wealth hubs around the world by Henley & Partners, West Palm Beach ranked No. 4, with a 112% spike in millionaires from 2014 through 2024. Only Scottsdale, Ariz., had a larger increase among U.S. cities over the same period.

The new Wells Fargo office is reportedly on track to open in August, and more staffers could eventually move there from their current locations, according to Bloomberg.

Barry Sommers, the head of the wealth unit, told Bloomberg that he and his wife made Palm Beach County their primary home last month. He noted that more of Wells Fargo's wealthiest clients are moving to south Florida and cited the ease of travel in the area.

"Wells Fargo Wealth & Investment Management is advancing its strategic commitment to serving high-- and ultrahigh--net--worth clients by increasing its presence in West Palm Beach," a spokeswoman tells Barron's Advisor. "Several senior leaders are relocating to the area, strengthening our local leadership presence, and deepening engagement with clients in this high-opportunity market," she adds.

The company declined to address specific questions about the move. Bloomberg reports that Wells Fargo will occupy 50,000 square feet in the One Flagler office building, on the same block as the offices of JPMorgan Chase and Citizens Financial Group.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Teledyne Sees Space Development Agency Wins as Key to More Golden Dome Contracts — Market Talk
data.description: Teledyne Technologies sees its Space Development Agency wins as a foothold and a springboard into broader missile defense opportunities in the U.S. CEO George Bobb says on an analyst call that the latest awards "continues our very strong participation across each of SDA's tracking layer programs, and positions us well for future Golden Dome related contracts," referring to the Trump administration's proposed homeland missile defense system. In 4Q the company was selected to supply space-based infrared detectors to the majority of prime contractors on the newly awarded U.S. Space Development Agency Tranche 3 Tracking Layer program. Bobb says the program is expected to scale, and could support its inclusion into future missile-defense work. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: Boralex Slides After Analysts Flag Softer Near-Term Output, Pricing Pressures
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Shares of Boralex were lower after analysts said softer generation and weaker French power prices are set to weigh on near-term results.

The stock was off by 3.7%, at 25.81 Canadian dollars (US$15.76), midday Wednesday. It has slipped about 1.4% over the past 12 months.

While long-term averages--which measure how actual power generation compares with typical historical conditions--have improved in markets such as Canada, these weren't enough to offset broader softness across the portfolio, National Bank of Canada analyst Baltej Sidhu said in a report.

"We saw strength in BLX's Canadian wind portfolio, however, it was insufficient to offset softer generation across the remainder of its portfolio," he said, cutting the stock's target price to C$39 from C$41. The stock's rating of "outperform" was unchanged.

The analyst reduced his 2026 forecast for earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, to C$720 million, down from C$754 million previously and below consensus estimates of C$778 million. He cited updated expectations for weaker French power prices--which hadn't been incorporated into previous outlooks--as well as Boralex's U.S. wind farms, which are expected to lose certain tax benefits.

Still, the company remains financially flexible, the analyst said, noting the C$811 million in liquidity as of the third quarter, as well as access to C$400 million in investment tax credits to tap into.

Additionally, he said that project ramp-ups could set the stage for near-term growth, including new battery and wind facilities coming online and several larger developments moving closer to final investment decisions.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Fed Governor Lisa Cook Likely To Keep Her Job After Supreme Court Arguments: Report
data.description:
Key points:
  • Kavanaugh’s comment to Solicitor General D. John Sauer was part of the hearing in the Supreme Court on whether Cook should be allowed to remain in office.
  • Donald Trump had first moved to fire Cook in August last year.
  • Lisa Cook was apparently one of the Federal Reserve governors who backed Powell in maintaining the interest rates.

Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh on Wednesday reportedly said that the Federal Reserve’s independence could be shattered if Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook gets fired without a judicial review, in a move that boosts her chances to stay in her position at the central bank. 

According to a report from CNBC, Kavanaugh’s comment to Solicitor General D. John Sauer was one of many skeptical questions to Sauer by the court’s justices as they heard arguments on whether Cook should be allowed to remain in office as she challenges in lower courts Trump’s attempt to fire her for uncharged allegations that she committed mortgage fraud.

“Your position that there’s no judicial review, no process required, no remedy available, very low bar for cause — that the President alone determines — and that would weaken, if not shatter, the independence of the Federal Reserve,” Kavanaugh reportedly told Sauer.

Trump’s Efforts To Fire Cook

Donald Trump had first moved to fire Cook in August last year, citing allegations made by Federal Housing Finance Director Bill Plute that Cook made false statements in applying for a home mortgage.

In response, Cook sued Trump in an attempt to block her removal from the Fed. A federal District Court judge in Washington had ruled that she can continue to remain with the central bank pending the outcome of her lawsuit.

Cook’s Support For Powell

Lisa Cook was apparently one of the Federal Reserve governors who backed Powell in maintaining the interest rates.

Federal Reserve has been facing fears surrounding its independence not only from attempts to fire Lisa Cook, but recent disclosure by Fed Chairman Jerome Powell that he is subject of a criminal investigation by the U.S. Attorney’s Office in Washington D.C., in connection with his testimony he gave last year on multi-billion-dollar renovation of the central bank’s headquarters there. 

Powell has said that the real reason for him to be under probe is the Fed maintaining interest rates steady last year, which has been criticized heavily by Trump. 

How Did Markets React?

U.S. equities edged higher on Wednesday. At the time of writing, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY), which tracks the S&P 500 index, was up 1%; the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) rise 0.3%; and the SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (DIA) was higher by 0.5%. Retail sentiment around the S&P 500 ETF on Stocktwits trended in the ‘bearish’ territory at the time of writing.

data.title: Thyssenkrupp Shares Rise as Defense Arm Vies for Canadian Military Contract
data.description:
By Joe Stonor

Shares in Thyssenkrupp and naval combat company TKMS rose after TKMS's CEO revealed details of the company's pitch for a lucrative Canadian military contract.

Stock in industrial company Thyssenkrupp, which spun off TKMS last year, closed 4.75% higher at 10.48 euros. TKMS, which has fewer shares in circulation, closed up 3.2% at 98.35 euros.

TKMS is vying with South Korean company Hanwha Ocean to supply Canada with 12 high-end submarines. The contract is estimated to be worth more than 10 billion euros ($11.7 billion), Reuters reported, citing unnamed sources.

Speaking to the outlet, TKMS Chief Executive Oliver Burkhard said the company's proposition goes beyond just submarines. It would amount to "a broad economic package," and could cover investment commitments in rare earths, mining artificial intelligence and automotive batteries, he said.

A TKMS spokesperson told Dow Jones Newswires that TKMS would make the submarines in Germany if successful in winning the contract, though this could change if the Canadian government required.

"We are able to respond flexible and quickly to all requirements of the Canadian government, also with regard to more local production," the spokesperson said.

Write to Joe Stonor at josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com

data.title: Microsoft 365 Services Are Down, As Per Outage Reports From Downdetector
data.description:
Several services of Microsoft (MSFT) 365, including Teams and Outlook, were down on Wednesday, according to the service-tracking website Downdetector.

Microsoft confirmed the outage in a post on X, stating that the company is investigating the issue affecting its Microsoft 365 services.

Later, the company said that its investigation indicates a possible third-party networking issue may be affecting access to its services, including Teams and Outlook, for some users.

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: Nymex Overview: Oil Futures Rise on Trump's Greenland Comments, Kazakhstan Outages — OPIS
data.description:
Crude oil contracts were reclaiming some of Tuesday's declines at midday Wednesday after President Trump ruled out the use of military forces to take control of Greenland.

The NYMEX March West Texas Intermediate contract was up 9cts to $60.45/bbl at about 11:30 a.m. ET and the April contract was 13cts higher at $60.22/bbl.

March ICE Brent futures were 6cts higher at $64.98/bbl and April Brent was up 14cts to $64.33/bbl.

Low temperatures across much of the U.S. continued to support diesel futures and the February ULSD contract was up 8.22cts to $2.4207/gal, with March adding 5.6cts to $2.3537/gal. Gasoline futures were also strengthening, with February RBOB contract up 2.18cts to $1.8457/gal and March 1.94cts higher at $1.8704/gal.

Petroleum futures found some support from reports that production at major oil fields in Kazakhstan, including Tengiz, was halted by power distribution issues, with output possibly halted for seven to ten days.

Exports through the Black Sea CPC pipeline have been affected, as some crude has been diverted to the domestic market. The operator declared force majeure on deliveries.

The International Energy Agency on Wednesday raised its forecast for global oil-demand growth in 2026 to roughly 930,000 b/d, up from its previous estimate of 860,000 b/d. IEA said world oil supply is still expected to exceed demand, with a significant surplus likely to persist into the first quarter, barring major disruptions.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

Reporting by Allegra Fradkin, afradkin@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeffrey Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com

data.title: REG - Jade Road Investmts. - Re-denomination of trading currency
data.description:
RNS Number : 8550P Jade Road Investments Limited 21 January 2026  

21 January 2026

JADE ROAD INVESTMENTS LIMITED

("Jade Road Investments", "JADE" or the "Company)

Re-denomination of trading currency

Jade Road Investments Limited (AIM:JADE), the London quoted investment company that looks to provide investors exposure to alternative investments which aim to offer higher returns and lower risk than traditional asset classes announces that the trading currency which the Company's ordinary shares of no par value are quoted in will change from British Pence (GBX) to United States Dollar (USD) from 8.00 a.m. on, 28 January 2026.

There will be no change to the Company's ISIN or SEDOL.

For further information, please contact:

Jade Road Investments Limited

+44 (0) 778 531 5588

John Croft

Zeus Capital Limited - Nominated Adviser

+44 (0) 203 829 5000

James Joyce / Andrew de Andrade
